post.title: The Post-Board Meeting Lock-In
post.body:
Last week we presented to the board and came away with a focused dose of reality. On one hand, the need for cost reduction was unavoidable due to the lack of new revenue to match our original plan. On the other hand, our focus with Canva and Jar overall was resoundingly accepted and supported.

More details below in the formal board recap!

week.text: Cost plan finalized with CFO Q1-2026
CEO Dashboard + Board recap to full team
Review new Jar roadmap with Arnoe

month.text: Revamp GTM with Canva + production lead
CHIPS Alpha launch to 20+ designers and 10 sample clients
AI for Operators course delivered to network

reading.title: 40 MCP Servers That Turn Claude Into a Productivity Machine
Introducing Canva AI 2.0: Reimagining how the world creates
Ethereum Whitepaper — Smart Contracts & Decentralized Apps
The 9 Levels of Leadership Every Founder Should Understand
The Most Boring Billion-Dollar Businesses of 2027

signals.text: Chapter Three locked. Three-lane model holds — Jar handed to Arnoe, personal focus consolidates on Subscriptions (Bryan) and CHIPS standup.
Canva QBR in Austin landed well. Packaging & pricing presentation to Meg Moss opened direct access to Canva's sales team.
Board meeting done. Chapter Three update dashboard live at russperry.github.io/dp-chapter-three/.
JAR AI go/no-go = go. 1/2/3 month roadmap now in build mode. Three external resources (Neutech, Irya, Svemir) active on the agentic track.
Consolidated Comms first live demo with Ops team — scope cut ~75%, right-sized to a single Client:DP comms channel. Delivery in progress.
Auto Assigner: all three improvements shipped — single queue, auto-run on give-back >30min, revision logic. Adobe contract negotiation ongoing.
Net MRR: -$43,943. Churn ($112K) outpacing new MRR ($71K). Churn reduction is the #1 lever. April demo completion rate: 80% (129/162).
Intercom renewal signed at significant discount. HubSpot negotiation ongoing — vendor not acknowledging team size constraints.
Investigating creative contractor COGS creep. Auditing ETL/Tableau pipeline — no single owner remains. First week in Ops OS.
Ops OS rolling out company-wide this week. Windows install path not yet usable end-to-end — running old Windows machine as test rig.
Ambral rollout: all accounts active, friction remains from Ambral/HubSpot duplication. Official rollout pending final refinements.
